About.metaDescription: Learn about WebAriadne — our story, values, and commitment to fresh groceries delivered with care.
About.title: About us
About.heroSubtitle: WebAriadne connects local producers and trusted brands with households who care about quality, value, and sustainability.
About.storyTitle: From neighbourhood market to your doorstep
About.storyP1: WebAriadne started with a simple idea: grocery shopping should feel personal, transparent, and convenient. We built an online store that celebrates seasonal produce, honest labels, and fair prices.
About.storyP2: Today we work with farmers, bakeries, and specialty suppliers across Slovakia — curating a catalog you can trust, whether you are stocking the pantry or planning a week of family meals.
About.valuesSubtitle: Every product on our shelves reflects the standards we hold ourselves to.
About.valueQualityDesc: We vet suppliers, check freshness, and stand behind every item we sell.
About.valueFreshDesc: We prioritise in-season produce and short supply chains wherever possible.
About.valueCommunityDesc: We support local growers and give back through food-bank partnerships.
About.missionTitle: Making good food accessible to everyone
About.missionDesc: Whether you shop weekly or need a last-minute top-up, our team is here to help — online, in store, and through our blog with recipes and tips.

ariadne-foods.description: Ariadne Foods is our flagship fresh and pantry partner, supplying produce, grains, and daily staples with a focus on traceability and fair pricing.

ariadne-roasters.description: Ariadne Roasters sources beans from sustainable farms and roasts in small batches for balanced, aromatic cups at home.

ariadne-teas.description: From everyday green tea to fragrant jasmine blends, Ariadne Teas curates leaves packed for freshness and clarity of flavour.

ariadne-pets.description: Ariadne Pets offers balanced dry food, treats, and wet meals formulated with named proteins and transparent ingredient lists.

starbucks.description: Starbucks brings café favourites to your kitchen with whole-bean and ground coffees roasted to their signature profiles.

stouffer.description: Stouffer supplies trusted dairy and chilled products for breakfasts, baking, and quick family meals.

wonder.description: Wonder delivers soft, versatile breads ideal for sandwiches, toast, and everyday baking needs.

lucia-h.name: Lucia H.
lucia-h.bio: Lucia shares practical batch-cooking plans for busy households.

seasonal-spring-produce.title: 5 spring vegetables to add to your cart this week
seasonal-spring-produce.excerpt: Bright, tender produce is back at the market — here is what to grab first and how to use it.
seasonal-spring-produce.content:
Spring is the easiest season to eat more vegetables. Crowns of broccoli, sweet cherry tomatoes, and crisp greens arrive with peak flavor and fair prices.

Start with one pan-roasted tray: toss broccoli and carrots with olive oil, salt, and lemon. Serve over grains or alongside grilled fish.

Shopping tip: choose firm stalks and deep green heads. Store produce unwashed in the crisper and rinse just before cooking to keep leaves crisp longer.

organic-pantry-essentials.title: Building an organic pantry on a budget
organic-pantry-essentials.excerpt: Stock versatile staples that stretch meals without stretching your grocery bill.
organic-pantry-essentials.content:
A smart pantry starts with grains, legumes, and spices you reach for every week. Organic quinoa, rice blends, and whole-grain pasta anchor quick dinners.

Buy larger bags of staples you use often and refill jars at home — it cuts packaging waste and per-serving cost.

Pair pantry bases with fresh market produce: a scoop of grains plus roasted vegetables becomes lunch in minutes.

coffee-at-home.title: Better coffee at home: grind, water, and timing
coffee-at-home.excerpt: Small changes to your routine can rival your favorite café cup.
coffee-at-home.content:
Freshly ground beans make the biggest difference. Grind right before brewing and match grind size to your method — finer for espresso, coarser for French press.

Use filtered water just off the boil and measure coffee by weight when you can. A simple scale keeps flavor consistent cup after cup.

Store beans in a cool, dark place in an airtight container. Avoid the freezer unless you are dividing bulk buys into single-use portions.

pet-food-guide.title: How to choose quality food for dogs and cats
pet-food-guide.excerpt: Labels can be confusing — focus on protein, portions, and your pet's life stage.
pet-food-guide.content:
Look for named protein sources first on the ingredient list and formulas matched to age, size, and activity level.

Wet food can support hydration; dry kibble is convenient for portion control. Many households combine both with guidance from their vet.

Transition slowly over seven to ten days when switching brands to avoid upset stomachs. Watch coat quality, energy, and appetite as signals.

meal-prep-basics.title: Meal prep basics for busy weekdays
meal-prep-basics.excerpt: One hour on Sunday can save you from weekday takeout — here is a simple plan.
meal-prep-basics.content:
Pick two proteins, two grains, and three vegetables you already enjoy. Roast trays together while rice or quinoa simmers on the stove.

Store components separately so textures stay fresh. Combine at lunch with a drizzle of dressing or sauce.

Cherry tomatoes, pre-washed greens, and hard-boiled eggs are fast finishers that make bowls feel complete without extra cooking.

reduce-food-waste.title: Simple ways to reduce food waste at home
reduce-food-waste.excerpt: Plan portions, store smarter, and use leftovers before they turn — small habits add up.
reduce-food-waste.content:
Shop with meals in mind instead of filling the cart. A short list tied to recipes prevents impulse buys that spoil first.

Move older items to the front of the fridge and label containers with dates. Citrus, apples, and root vegetables last longer in cool, dry storage.

Freeze bread ends for croutons, simmer vegetable scraps into stock, and turn ripe fruit into smoothies or compote before it softens.
